Appearances:
Gene Benitez appeared via zoom for Juan Rivera (Administrator).

Discussion was held regarding the violence distribution that had been set aside in its entirety, that counsel made a side agreement to have the fees extraordinary fees paid, that $780.00 is owed to counsel, and that the court suggested filing a small claims matter to recover the $780.00 since it was a side agreement between both parties. The matter is continued to November 17, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Josh Fredricks. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-08-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gener Benitez appeared by zoom for Juan Rivera (administrator).

Mr. Benitez stated that this has been a case of broken promises, that the administrator paid the statutory fees, that they had agreed that he would pay the extraordinary fees of $3,000.00 with respect to the motion to set aside the order for final distribution, and that now they are hearing of payment of $200.00 a month. Discussion was held regarding a lien on the property. Mr. Benitez expressed concern about a lien stating that they don't know if the property will ever get sold. Status of the petition for final distribution is off calendar. The Petition to Compel Administrator to Pay the Statutory Fees, etc. is continued to July 8, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Mr. Benitez time to negotiate more with his client.
Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-25-2023 Continued To Jan-08-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Gener Benitez, Esq. for Juan Rivera, Administrator.

Mr. Benitez stated that Mr. Rivera has paid some fees, that he still owes $4,300.00 under the current pay plan, that they should be paid in full by December 8, 2023, and that he would like a continuance. The Court addressed Mr. Benitez stating that it appears that now he is technically or potentially a creditor. Brief discussions are held. The Court noted that the fees would come out of the sale of the property. Mr. Benitez stated that Mr. Rivera does not wish to sell the residence. The Court addressed the extraordinary fees. Mr. Rivera stated that they discounted the extraordinary fees and that Mr. Rivera agreed to pay $2000.00. The Court stated that the goal is to get this estate closed.

Both the status on the filing of a petition for final distribution and the petiton by the Ibarra Professional Law Corporation to compel the administrator to pay the statutory fees are continued January 8, 2024, at 9:00 a.m. Dept. 204.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-01-2021 Continued To Oct-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gene Benitez, via ZOOM, for Juan Rivera, Personal Representative.

The court begins by confirming that counsel has seen the most recent Examiner's notes. Mr. Benitez reports that the matter is not ready to close as there is an issue with obtaining an EIN number from the IRS and he has found it difficult to contact the IRS for assistance. Mr. Benitez reports however that a status petition is almost ready, but that he has been unable to contact his client to obtain a signature to verify the petition. The court then confirms with Mr. Benitez that Daniel Loyola, Giovanni Sampedro and Omar Sampedro do not have children.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to 10/13/2021 at 9:00 am, in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Mar-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Gene Benitez for and with Juan Rivera.

Mr. Benitez reported that his client has some money raised, that Mr. Rivera's kids are helping him, that they know he needs to raise $14,000.00+, that his client is 64 years old, is getting Social Security and should be able to raise the money needed. The court asked whether there is a solid plan and noted that probate fees will need to be paid. Mr. Benitez asked for more time, stating that the EIN issues need to be resolved before they can file appropriate returns, that there was a delay because of COVID and the application got lost, the application sent by mail to IRS in October was not received so they faxed it on Feb. 17, 2021 with authorization from IRS, that it was not available to be submitted online, that IRS used to take 30 days but because of COVID it could take up to 90 days. Mr. Benitez further stated that the property belongs to the sister, that they don't have a problem getting a waiver from the sister, and that the brother already owns half. The court noted that it is firm on final distribution to be completed in 6 months, that it would be up to counsel and his client to come up with plan B if the $15,000.00 is not raised for the estate to close within this calendar year.

The report of status of administration and petition for more time to close is taken under submission for a declaration to be filed within a week and a copy of the waiver to be submitted.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-05-2020 Continued To Nov-23-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gene Benitez, Esq., counsel for Juan Rivera, Administrator (appearing by video conference).

Mr. Benitez reports that the estate was in a position to be closed until it was discovered that the decedent had not filed income tax returns for a period of time. The Court advises counsel that the current practice now is to file a Report of Status and Petition for Additional Time to Administer Estate rather than just appear at hearing.

The Court continues the matter to November 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. If counsel has filed the Report of Status and Petition for Additional Time to Administer Estate by that date, no appearance is necessary.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Court Examiner: Helen Yune Trowbridge; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. Bailiff: Deputy Johnson. (D204)
